name: The Joint Chiropractic

imputed_royalty_fee.trigger: After Required Opening Date if not open

liquidated_damages.formula: avg monthly (royalty + brand fund) x lesser of 24 or months remaining

raw.gag_clause_quote: current and former franchisees sign provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience with us

provisions.burden: License to use Marks and System to operate one Clinic from accepted site. Not a sale of territory rights.
10-year initial term from effective date.

One 10-year renewal. Must sign then-current FA (may be materially different), pay 25% of then-current IFF, sign general release, remodel, upgrade FFE, extend lease. If no mutual agreement on further renewals, franchise ends after renewal term.
Greater of 7% of Gross Sales or $700/month. Bimonthly reporting and payment. 2025 incentive: 3%/5%/7% ramp-up for new clinics.
Currently 2%, up to 3% on 30 days notice. No voting rights.
$599/month, up to $799 on 30 days notice.
$100 flat + 18% per annum (10% in CA). $35 NSF fee per incident.
Must authorize ACH debit for all amounts owed (ATTACHMENT E). Must maintain sufficient funds.
Franchisee must identify site within Site Selection Area. Franchisor acceptance required (15 business days).
Must use franchisor's Clinic Design. Must hire licensed architect. Plans must be approved. Minimum $20,000 every 4 years for remodel.
Must open by Required Opening Date. Imputed Royalty Fees of $700/month if delayed.
Managing Owner and General Manager must complete initial training. Post-opening training up to $1,000/person/day. Remedial training if clinic not in compliance.
Managing Owner required (5%+ ownership). Either MO or trained GM onsite during business hours.
Must comply with Manual, maintain equipment, follow pricing guidelines, purchase from approved suppliers, maintain HIPAA compliance.
May terminate if franchisor fails to cure material breach within 90 days. Must still comply with post-term obligations (excl. liquidated damages).
10 days: monetary, insurance, credentialing. 20 days: license maintenance. 90 days: failure to open (max 180 after Required Opening Date). 30 days: other.

Insolvency, bankruptcy, seizure, abandonment (3 consecutive business days), loss of license, criminal conviction, material law violation, adverse reputation acts, health/safety hazards, material misrepresentations, unauthorized transfers, IP violations, 3+ defaults in 12 months, cross-default.
If business model deemed unlawful in franchisee's state and parties can't agree on fix. No liquidated damages if franchisee signs Mutual Termination and Release.

Transferee must: apply, qualify, train, assume obligations, sign then-current FA, remodel within 6 months, pay transfer fee ($5K DC Path / $15K other). Seller must: be in compliance, assign lease, sign general release. 16 conditions total.
No approval needed for: <5% ownership, family/trust, between owners, to 100% owned entity. Must give 10 days notice and pay $2,500.
Franchisor has 30-day evaluation period to match any bona fide offer. Can substitute cash for any payment form. Reinstated if material change to offer terms or sale not completed within 120 days.
No involvement in 'competing business' — defined as private-pay, non-insurance chiropractic under membership model deriving $10K+/year. Does NOT restrict general chiropractic practice.
2 years after termination/expiration. No competing business at clinic premises or within 10 miles of your clinic or ANY other clinic in the system. Very broad geographic scope.

All owners and spouses of owners must sign Franchise Owner Agreement. Includes brand protection covenants, transfer restrictions, and FINANCIAL ASSURANCE for all obligations. Spouse liable even without ownership interest.

Spousal Liability explicitly flagged as special risk: 'Your spouse must sign a document that makes your spouse liable for all financial obligations under the franchise agreement even though your spouse has no ownership interest.'

On termination for default or unauthorized termination: Average monthly (royalty + brand fund) for preceding 12 months × lesser of 24 or months remaining in term. If paid timely, franchisor will not pursue lost profits (but may seek other damages).
Mandatory pre-suit mediation (except IP/noncompete). Mediation and litigation in Maricopa County, AZ (subject to state law).
Arizona law governs (subject to state law and federal trademark law).
Mutual waiver of jury trial (subject to state law).

Must maintain: all-risk property (replacement value), CGL ($1M/$3M), auto ($1M/$3M), malpractice ($1M/$3M), commercial umbrella ($1M/$3M), business interruption (6 months), employer's liability, workers comp, landlord-required, licensure-required. A-VIII+ rated carrier. 30 days notice to franchisor.

Broad indemnification: clinic operations, chiropractic services, breach of agreement, breach of third-party contracts, failure to name franchisor as insured, representations to transferees, tax penalties, libel/slander, labor/employment claims. Franchisor controls defense. Survives termination.
Limited: only for claims that franchisee's use of Marks in strict compliance infringes third-party IP. Requires full compliance with all agreements.

Remove trade dress, alter premises, cease IP use (including software), return Manual/branded materials, delete social media, assign phone numbers/domain names/listings, send patient list and records (subject to privacy laws), cancel fictitious names, comply with data retention, pay amounts due including liquidated damages.
Franchisor has option to purchase clinic at expiration or termination. Must sign Asset Purchase Agreement (Exhibit H-5).
Neither party liable for delays due to Force Majeure (fire, flood, earthquake, strike, pandemic, government order, etc.). Must resume performance within reasonable time.

officer_bankruptcy.case: Case No. 20-31318, Northern District of Texas
officer_bankruptcy.note: Not related to The Joint Chiropractic.

cases.name: Carmel Mountain et al. v. The Joint
cases.allegations: Breach of contract, breach of implied covenant, wrongful termination, fraud, promissory fraud, negligent misrepresentation, CA Franchise Investment Law violations
cases.resolution: Settled December 12, 2016. $800,000 total ($600K insurance, $100K stock). Mutual general releases. Dismissed with prejudice Dec 20, 2016.

validation_questions: Confirm Item 19 cohort definition with franchisor.

internal_unresolveds.reason: Item 21 auditor and opinion type unknown — financial statements are image-only
Item 21 financial metrics (total assets, liabilities, equity, revenue, net income) not extractable from text
Going concern status unknown without auditor's report
Franchise agreement not fully clause-walked — covered via Item 17 cross-references
Net Profit sample bias — only 406 of 785 qualifying clinics (51.7%) provided P&L data
Maturity bias in quartiles — Q1 avg 97.6 months vs Q4 avg 48.8 months
